source: European Social Survey Round 10 (2020/2021) — Italian subsample

self_direction.label: Self-Direction
self_direction.description: Independent thought and action — choosing, creating, exploring

stimulation.label: Stimulation
stimulation.description: Excitement, novelty, and challenge in life

hedonism.label: Hedonism
hedonism.description: Pleasure and sensuous gratification for oneself
items: ipgdtim (important to have a good time)

power.label: Power
power.description: Social status and prestige, control over people and resources

security.label: Security
security.description: Safety, harmony, and stability of society, relationships, self
items: impsafe (important to live in secure surroundings)

conformity.label: Conformity
conformity.description: Restraint of actions likely to upset or harm others, social norms

tradition.label: Tradition
tradition.description: Respect and acceptance of customs, culture, religion

benevolence.label: Benevolence
benevolence.description: Preservation and enhancement of welfare of close others
items: iplylfr (important to be loyal to friends)

universalism.label: Universalism
universalism.description: Understanding, appreciation, tolerance for all people and nature
items: ipeqopt (important that people are treated equally)

openness_to_change.description: Emphasizes independence of thought and readiness for new experience

conservation.description: Emphasizes self-restriction, order, and resistance to change

self_enhancement.description: Emphasizes pursuit of one's own success and dominance

self_transcendence.description: Emphasizes concern for the welfare of others and nature

profile_interpretation.summary: Italians show a strong orientation toward caring for others (benevolence, universalism) and personal security, combined with valuing independent thought. They place relatively low emphasis on power-seeking and thrill-seeking. This profile is consistent with a family-oriented, community-minded culture with strong social bonds and moderate risk aversion.
